notebooks.execution_method: in-process sequential executor because the automation sandbox blocks local Jupyter TCP kernel connections
key_checks: all 14 code cells executed in order with zero unexpected exceptions
PFCT, Chung-dense, and friction-theory conductivity predictions were finite and physically plausible
PFCT pressure and temperature sweeps were smooth and monotonic over the stated single-phase range
methane-gas and n-heptane-liquid reference deviations were below 20 percent
thermal diffusivity, Prandtl, Reynolds, Nusselt, and heat-transfer calculations were finite and positive
notebook JSON, nbformat, equations, Colab badge, and introduced documentation links were validated
notebooks.summary: Removed the obsolete neqsim 2.5.35 pin; corrected conductivity terminology; documented current API initialization and supported model choices; added reproducible gas, pure-component, pressure, temperature, composition, and heat-transfer studies; added deterministic plots, engineering interpretation, limitations, exercises, and executable validation checks.
